response: The Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), founded in 1861, is ranked the #1 university in the world, with a highly selective acceptance rate of just 3.9%. It enrolls approximately 11,500 students, employs 1,069 faculty members, and boasts an impressive 98 Nobel laureates among its affiliates, supported by a $24.6 billion endowment. Annual tuition costs $57,590, reflecting its status as a premier institution in science, technology, and research.
